Then, following a novel meta optimization scheme to optimize the model to obtain good testing performance on the virtual testing sets after training on the virtual training set, our framework can effectively drive the model to better capture semantics and visual representations of individual concepts.

Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework.

Each testing set of the RefCOCO dataset is split to evaluate the models' performance on compositions seen and unseen during training.
Novel compositions of learned concepts present a challenge for the models' generalization capability.
The performance of models on subsets containing novel compositions drops significantly compared to those with compositions seen during training, with a performance gap of 14%−17%.
The performance gap between the two subsets can reach 14%-17%, measured by the metric of overall IoU.
Such a clear performance gap indicates that existing models struggle with generalizing to novel compositions of learned concepts.
Existing models struggle with generalizing due to not effectively capturing the semantics and visual representations of individual concepts during training.

node_2: meta compositional referring expression segmentation (mcres) framework
edge: From the perspective of meta learning, a Meta Compositional Referring Expression Segmentation (MCRES) framework is proposed to effectively handle the challenging problem by changing the model training scheme.
Meta learning proposes to perform virtual testing during model training for better performance.
MCRES framework incorporates a meta optimization scheme that consists of three steps: virtual training, virtual testing, and meta update.
Both virtual testing and meta update are activities related to data evaluation and modification in the context of virtual testing and training.
The virtual training set is used to construct a virtual training model in the context of virtual training and testing.
The virtual testing set is constructed by splitting the virtual training set and contains novel compositions not present in the training set.
Data samples in the virtual testing set contain novel compositions compared to the virtual training set.
The model is trained using the virtual training set and evaluated on the virtual testing set in the context of virtual training.
During virtual training, the model may learn the compositions of individual concepts as a whole without truly understanding their semantics.
Training samples in the virtual training set contain compositions like 'yellow banana' which the model can superficially learn.
